story.problem: Writing raw SQL strings in Python means string concatenation (and SQL injection risks), database-specific syntax that breaks when you switch engines, and no way to express relationships between tables as Python objects. SQLAlchemy solved this by offering both a high-level ORM and a Pythonic SQL expression layer.
story.mental_model: SQLAlchemy has two layers you use together. The Core layer gives you a SQL expression API — table.select().where(table.c.id == 5) generates correct SQL for any database. The ORM layer maps Python classes to tables, so you work with User objects and SQLAlchemy handles the INSERT/SELECT/JOIN. You can mix both layers freely.
story.when_to_use: Use SQLAlchemy in any Python application that talks to a relational database — web apps, data pipelines, ETL scripts. It supports PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite, and Oracle, so the same code runs everywhere. It's the standard ORM in the Python ecosystem, with integrations for Flask, FastAPI, and most web frameworks.
story.when_not_to_use: SQLAlchemy's full ORM is overkill for simple read-only queries or scripts that just need to run a few SQL statements. For those cases, use the database's driver directly (psycopg2, sqlite3) or a lightweight query builder like databases.

alternatives.name: tortoise-orm
alternatives.reason: Async-native ORM modelled after Django ORM; better for pure asyncio applications

alternatives.name: peewee
alternatives.reason: Smaller, simpler ORM for straightforward CRUD applications without complex relationships

alternatives.name: databases
alternatives.reason: Async SQL query library without ORM overhead; good when you want to write SQL directly but asynchronously

nodes.label: sqlalchemy
nodes.summary: Import from sqlalchemy. Two APIs: Core (SQL builder) and ORM (object mapping).

nodes.label: create_engine(url)
nodes.summary: Database connection factory: `engine = create_engine('postgresql://...')`. Manages connection pool.

nodes.label: Session / sessionmaker
nodes.summary: ORM unit-of-work. Use with `with Session(engine) as session:`. Tracks changes, flushes, commits.

nodes.label: DeclarativeBase
nodes.summary: ORM model base: `class MyModel(Base): __tablename__ = 'table'`. Maps Python class to DB table.

nodes.label: Column / mapped_column
nodes.summary: Define table columns: `Column(String, primary_key=True)` or typed `mapped_column(int, nullable=False)`.

nodes.label: session.execute() / conn.execute()
nodes.summary: Run a statement. `result = session.execute(stmt)`. Use .scalars(), .all(), .one() to fetch.

nodes.label: Alembic (migrations)
nodes.summary: SQLAlchemy pairs with Alembic for schema migrations: alembic init, revision --autogenerate, upgrade.

nodes.label: Async (create_async_engine)
nodes.summary: from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import create_async_engine, AsyncSession. Full async support.
